turns.user_message: They casually mentioned that I always seem to overthink the smallest details, and it really stuck with me all day. It only happened a few days ago, but the comment just keeps echoing in my head. I found myself second-guessing every little choice I made after that. It feels weirdly heavy to carry around a simple observation like that. I just wish I could shake off this sudden self-doubt before it ruins my whole evening.

That same remark keeps popping up every time we interact now, turning normal conversations into these exhausting minefields. I catch myself pausing before I speak, constantly filtering my thoughts so I do not sound too intense or dramatic. It has become this quiet routine where I shrink my own reactions just to keep the peace. I hate how quickly I am adjusting my natural rhythm to match their expectations. The tension just lingers in the air now, making every exchange feel like walking on thin ice.

I completely lost it yesterday when they brought it up again, and I realized I have been holding my breath around this person for weeks. Every single word I say now gets dissected in my mind before it ever leaves my mouth. I am so exhausted from trying to prove I am not the problem that I barely recognize my own voice anymore. The constant scrutiny has drained all the warmth out of our connection, leaving just this hollow, heavy ache. I feel completely trapped inside my own head, terrified that one wrong move will shatter everything we built.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This just started recently.
